confusable_groups.note: 再也 requires an explicit negative (再也不/没); 不再 already contains the negation and stands alone
有点儿/有点 lean negative/complaint; 有一点儿 is neutral-emphatic; 稍微 is neutral and milder; 略微 is formal/literary
差一点儿/差点儿 = narrowly avoided an outcome; 几乎 = close to complete/total; 将近 = close to a quantity (number/amount)
成人 is formal/written (legal, official contexts); 大人 is colloquial/spoken, used esp. when talking to children
依照 = according to a standard/procedure (formal); 按照 = according to a rule/plan (neutral); 据 = according to a source/information (can introduce evidence)
倒是 softens or concedes with mild contrast; 反倒 stresses an unexpected reversal (colloquial); 相反 is a neutral adjective/connector meaning 'opposite'
全体 = the whole group/collective (noun-like, e.g. 全体成员); 全部 = the entire amount/all of it (noun modifier); 全都 = adverb 'all without exception' (modifies verbs)
古 = ancient as attributive morpheme/adj (古城); 古老 = age-old (people/things still existing); 古代 = noun 'ancient times'; 悠久 = long-standing (history/tradition, formal praise)
烦 = feel irritated or to bother sb (verb/adj, surface reaction); 烦恼 = inner vexation, worried trouble (deeper emotional state); 恼火 = angry-annoyed (stronger, outward irritation)
整天 = neutral 'all day long'; 成天 = colloquial, often implies complaint or tedium
好多 = colloquial 'a lot'; 许多 = neutral written 'many'; 不少 = litotes 'quite a few' (understatement); 众多 = formal 'numerous' (large collectives)
老是 = colloquial 'always keeps doing' (complaint tone); 总 = general 'always/overall'; 向来 = habitual 'has always been (this way)'; 历来 = historical precedent 'has always been (by tradition)'
独自 = alone, by oneself (manner adverb, emphasizes solitude); 单独 = separately/individually (can modify nouns or verbs, emphasizes separation from a group)
胡同儿/胡同 are Beijing erhua variants denoting the traditional hutong alley (culturally specific); 巷 is a general literary word for any narrow lane or alley
不一会儿 = colloquial 'in a short while' (narrative); 片刻 = literary/formal 'a moment, brief spell'
此后 = formal/written 'from this point onward; hereafter' (often opens a new phase); 之后 = general neutral 'after; afterwards' (everyday use)
气温 = specifically atmospheric/air temperature (weather/meteorology); 温度 = temperature in any context (body, water, oven, etc.)
老是 = colloquial, implies complaint/annoyance; 向来 = habitual pattern (neutral); 总 = neutral general always; 历来 = by historical precedent (formal)
古 = attributive prefix/adj 'ancient' (used in compounds); 古老 = age-old (descriptive adj); 古代 = noun 'ancient times'; 悠久 = long-standing (only of tradition/history)
烦 = mildly annoyed or to bother others; 烦恼 = inwardly troubled/vexed (sustained inner state); 恼火 = openly angry/irritated (more intense, outward)
夜里 = colloquial spoken 'at night'; 夜间 = formal/written 'nighttime' (used in official/technical contexts); 夜 = literary/classical monosyllable
那会儿 = colloquial Beijing er-hua; 那时候 = neutral spoken; both mean 'at that time' but register differs
这时 = written/narrative 'at this point'; 这时候 = colloquial spoken equivalent; interchangeable in meaning, differ in register
一带 = vague 'the area around X' (imprecise, spoken); 区 = administrative district; 地区 = region (geographic/political); 区域 = defined zone (more abstract/technical)
尽可能 = 'as far as possible' (emphasizes feasibility limit); 尽量 = 'as much as possible' (emphasizes maximizing effort); overlap is large but 尽量 is shorter and more colloquial
志愿 = aspiration + concrete act of volunteering; 抱负 = high ambition (formal, no action); 愿望 = personal wish or desire (emotional, not necessarily acted on)
宝宝 = affectionate term for young child (spoken); 宝贝 = broader 'treasure/darling' (child or partner); 娃娃 = child or doll; 婴儿 = formal 'infant/newborn' (clinical)
听众 = audience that listens (radio, concert, lecture); 观众 = audience that watches (TV, theater, sports); distinguish by sense organ
到来 = arrival of events/seasons/eras (often abstract); 到达 = neutral physical arrival at a place; 抵达 = formal written arrival; 来到 = arrive toward the speaker/scene
不良 = formal/written 'bad; harmful' (behavior, influence, records); 坏 = general colloquial 'bad; broken/spoiled'; 不良 never used for broken objects
秋季 = formal/written 'autumn season' (used in official, sports, or compound contexts); 秋 = general spoken/written 'autumn', also used in idioms
独自 = 'by oneself' emphasizing solitude/aloneness; 单独 = 'alone; separately' emphasizing separation or doing sth apart from others
后面 = neutral spatial 'behind'; 背后 = behind s.o.'s back (literal or figurative scheming); 后头 = colloquial synonym of 后面; 后 = particle/literary 'after; behind'
下边 = below/underneath (neutral spoken); 底下 = directly underneath (physical, colloquial); 下面 = below (also 'the following' in discourse); 以下 = below a threshold or 'hereafter' (formal written)
黑色 = black as a color noun (colour category); 黑 = black/dark as predicate adjective; 乌黑 = jet-black (lustrous, used of hair/eyes)
蓝色 = blue as color noun; 蓝 = standard blue adjective; 青 = blue-green/cyan (traditional color term, also means 'young')
美好 = beautiful/wonderful applied to abstract things (life, future, memories); 美丽 = physically beautiful (appearance); 美 = root adjective (beautiful) or abbreviation for America
盒 = small box/case (used as classifier 一盒); 盒子 = small box (standalone noun); 箱 = large box/trunk/suitcase
男孩儿 = young boy (child); 男生 = male student/boy (school context); 少年 = teenage youth (10–16); 小伙子 = young man/lad (informal); 青年 = young adult (18–35, formal/official)
基本上 = purely adverbial 'basically; on the whole'; 基本 = 'basic' (adjective) or 'basically' (adverb) — more versatile but less emphatic as adverb
灾害 = disaster as a noun (objective); 受灾 = be hit by a disaster (formal verb, official/news register); 遭殃 = suffer a calamity (colloquial, more dramatic/personal)
能够 = neutral modal 'can; be able to'; 得以 = formal/literary 'manage to; be enabled to' — implies a prior condition made it possible, cannot start a sentence alone
以外 = 'besides; outside of' (common in speech and writing after noun phrases); 之外 = same meaning but more formal/written register
两边 = both sides physically (spatial); 双方 = both parties (people or groups in an interaction or negotiation)
眼前 = before one's eyes / spatially in front (can mean 'right now'); 眼下 = right now (spoken, informal); 目前 = at present (neutral formal time adverb); 当前 = current/pressing (formal, implies urgency)
年前 = the period before New Year (approaching the holiday); 年初 = the beginning of the year (just after New Year); learners confuse these as both concern the New Year boundary
胡子 = beard/mustache (everyday colloquial); 胡须 = beard/whiskers (more formal or written register)

大胆 = bold/daring (neutral to mildly pejorative — can imply recklessness); 勇于 = have the courage to do X (positive, always tied to a specific admirable action); 豪迈 = bold-spirited/heroic (literary, broad character trait)
箱 is a large rigid container (trunk, chest, suitcase body); 盒子 is a small box or case (jewellery box, gift box). Size and sturdiness distinguish them.

男孩儿 = young child (boy, no school connotation); 男生 = male student/schoolboy (academic context); 少年 = adolescent boy 10–16 (literary/official); 小伙子 = young lad/guy (colloquial, late teens–20s); 青年 = young adult 18–35 (neutral, often collective).
大脑 = anatomical brain (cerebrum, medical/scientific); 头脑 = intellect/judgement (figurative, somewhat formal: 头脑清醒); 脑子 = brains/mind in everyday colloquial speech (用脑子想想).
牌子 is the colloquial spoken form for brand or sign (名牌子); 牌 is the base form and also covers playing cards and game tiles — wider range than 牌子.
公共汽车 is the standard mainland Chinese term for city bus; 巴士 is a loanword used in Hong Kong/Taiwan and for intercity coaches in the mainland — more informal or regional.
商务 = business in a formal/official sense (商务舱, 商务部); 商业 = commerce as an industry or sector; 生意 = everyday running of a trade or shop; 买卖 = the concrete act of buying and selling (transactions).

变为 = transform into (formal/written); 变成 = turn into (neutral, result-focused); 变得 = become + adj (only used before adjectives); 变 = to change/shift (bare verb); 成为 = become in the sense of taking on a role/status; 成 = succeed/become (short, often in complements like 变成).
照相机 is the full traditional form (photo-taking machine); 相机 is the shortened modern everyday form. 相机 also informally covers digital cameras and some video cameras.

不能不 = logical double negation 'cannot not do' (moral/logical compulsion, slightly emphatic); 不得不 = have no choice but (external circumstances force it); 不免 = unavoidably/can't help but (natural, emotionally toned); 势必 = bound to/inevitably (predicting an outcome, formal).
证 = an ID document or pass (身份证); also a bound morpheme 'to certify'; 证明 = to prove something / a letter of proof (less formal document); 证书 = a formal certificate, diploma, or credentials.
名人 = famous or notable person in any field (history, politics, sport, arts); 明星 = star/celebrity, typically in entertainment (film, music, sport).
明亮 = physically bright, well-lit (describes a room, light source); 明 = bright/clear in a broader sense, including clarity of understanding (明白) and as a literary adjective.

变为 = become/turn into (directional result); 变 = general change/become (simple verb); 改变 = change intentionally (transitive, deliberate); 变化 = change/variation (noun/verb, natural process); 变动 = fluctuation/shift (noun, often institutional or unstable)
孩子 = child/kid (neutral everyday); 小朋友 = child/little friend (warm, adult addressing a child); 儿童 = children (formal/official, e.g. 儿童节); 小孩 = kid (colloquial, slightly diminutive)
子女 = sons and daughters (neutral formal, legal/statistical contexts); 儿女 = sons and daughters (slightly more literary/emotional register)
字 = single character/letter (unit of script); 汉字 = Chinese character specifically; 词 = word as a lexical unit (may be multi-character); 文字 = writing system/script (collective, abstract)
胸 = chest (bare root, used in compounds); 胸部 = chest area (clinical/anatomical, with 部 suffix); 胸膛 = chest/bosom (physical cavity, more literary/vivid)
证 = certificate/proof (root morpheme, informal shorthand); 证明 = to prove; a written proof or attestation (verb+noun dual); 证书 = certificate as a formal document/credential (noun only)
公交车 = city transit bus (urban public transport system); 大巴 = coach/large bus (intercity or hired, not a scheduled city line)
华语 = Chinese language (term used in SE Asia diaspora communities); 语言 = language in general (abstract noun); 语 = language/speech (suffix/root in compounds); 母语 = native/mother tongue (any language)
班级 = class as an administrative unit (the homeroom group); 课堂 = classroom/class as the instructional space or session
寒冷 = cold/frigid (general descriptive adj); 严寒 = bitter/severe cold (stronger, literary); 炎热 = scorching hot (weather, intense)
颜色 = color (neutral everyday noun); 色 = color (root morpheme, used in compounds, also literary); 色彩 = color with tonal/figurative nuance (also: flavor, hue in art/rhetoric)
闭幕式 = closing ceremony (specific); 开幕式 = opening ceremony (specific); 典礼 = formal solemn ceremony (graduation, state); 仪式 = ritual/rite (broader, including religious or symbolic acts)
既然 = since/given that (colloquial logic, spoken); 鉴于 = in view of/given that (formal written, official/legal register)
好友 = close personal friend (emotional intimacy, non-family); 亲人 = close relatives/loved ones (family bond); 伙伴 = companion/partner (cooperative relationship, not necessarily intimate)
巧 = coincidentally; also means skillful (dual meaning, context-dependent); 正好 = just right/as it happens (timing/fit without the skillful nuance)
煤 = coal (common root word, everyday use); 煤炭 = coal (fuller formal/industrial term, e.g. in reports or energy sector)
外套 = coat/jacket (general outerwear, any material); 羽绒服 = down jacket (specific insulated garment type)
色 is a morpheme/literary noun; 颜色 is the everyday neutral noun for 'color'; 彩色 means 'multicolored/color' as an attribute (彩色电视); 色彩 (5k) is literary/figurative 'hue/flavor'
凉 is mildly cool/cold (pleasant or neutral); 寒冷 is unpleasantly cold (weather adjective); 严寒 (5k) is literary 'bitter/severe cold'; 冻 (5k) is the physical sensation/action of freezing
潮湿 (5k) is the neutral adjective for damp/humid; 潮 alone adds connotations of excessive or unpleasant moisture (also used colloquially for 'trendy')

脸色 = facial color/complexion or mood-signaling look; 气色 (5k) = overall complexion/vitality; 神色 (5k) = expression/air (more literary); 神情 (5k) = mental/emotional look; 表情 (5k) = facial expression (deliberate/mimicked)
常见 = often encountered (frequency of sighting); 常用 = frequently used in practice; 普通 (5k) = ordinary/unremarkable (neutral quality judgment); 罕见 (5k) = seldom seen (opposite anchor)
音乐会 covers any music concert (instrumental or vocal); 演唱会 is specifically a vocal/singing concert or pop/rock live show
喜剧 = comedy as a dramatic genre (film/stage); 相声 = traditional Chinese two-person comic dialogue (performance art); 漫画 (5k) = visual comic/manga/cartoon
深 (5k) = physically deep or dark (color); 深厚 = deep and solid (feelings, foundation, skills); 深沉 (5k) = deeply reserved or brooding (personality/mood)
正好 (5k) = exactly right / as it happens (timing or fit); 巧 emphasizes lucky coincidence ('what a coincidence') and also means skillful — context disambiguates
院子 = an enclosed courtyard/yard (residential); 院 = courtyard morpheme, also suffix for institutions (医院, 法院); 花园 (5k) = a planted garden, not just a bare yard
稻谷 (5k) = unhusked paddy rice (field/raw grain); 米 (5k) = husked uncooked rice (also 'meter'); 米饭 = cooked/steamed rice served as food
开水 (5k) = boiled water (hot or cooled but safe); 凉水 = cool/unboiled water (raw tap water, not safe to drink by Chinese convention)

赔 = to compensate someone for a loss or sell at a loss; 损失 (5k) = loss/damage (the noun); 亏损 (5k) = financial deficit (business accounting); 吃亏 (5k) = to come out worse off, be at a disadvantage (colloquial)
价格 (5k) = market price of a specific item; 物价 = commodity price level in general (macro); 代价 (5k) = figurative cost/sacrifice; 降价 = the action of cutting the price
内容 (5k) = content as subject matter/substance of a text or topic; 含量 = measurable amount/proportion of a component (e.g. sugar content, nutrient content)
爸 is the everyday informal word for 'dad'; 父亲 (5k) is the formal/written term for 'father'
潮 is colloquial/spoken for damp; 潮湿 is the standard written compound for humid/damp conditions
黑暗 is a noun/strong adj (physical or moral darkness); 暗 is a general adj (dim, also covert/secret)
深厚 = deep bonds/emotions (relationships only); 深 = general adj (depth, dark colour); 深沉 = brooding/reserved personality
难题 = a specific hard problem (noun); 难度 = abstract difficulty level (measure); 困难 = general hardship/difficulty (noun or adj)
密 adj = dense OR covert; 秘密 = secret (noun/adj, purely about secrecy); 保密 = the act of keeping something secret
存款 = bank savings/deposit (noun); 存 = verb to deposit or store; 押金 = refundable security deposit, not savings
研制 = R&D-based creation of a specific product; 开发 = exploit/open up (resources, software, land); 发展 = grow/expand something already existing
辞典 = dictionary of words and phrases; 字典 = dictionary of individual characters (by stroke/radical)
送到 = physically deliver to a destination; 捎 = deliver/bring along en passant; 转达 = relay a verbal or written message via a third party
图画 = hand-drawn or painted picture (artwork); 图片 = photo or printed/digital image; 图 = functional visual: diagram, chart, or map
灾 often functions as a morpheme in compounds (灾区, 救灾); 灾害 is the standard standalone formal noun for disaster
院长 = head of an institute/hospital/college (院); 校长 = head of a school or university (校); 主任 = head of a department or office
远处 = location noun (the distant place/afar); 遥远 = adjective describing something as remote/far-off
电子版 = specifically the electronic/digital format; 版本 = any version or edition regardless of medium
图画 = hand-drawn artwork; 图片 = photo or digital image; 图 = generic diagram/map/drawing
驾照 = driver's license only; 执照 = any business/professional permit or license
早晨 = early morning (everyday, colloquial); 清晨 = early morning (literary, poetic register)

早期 = relatively early stage (not necessarily the very beginning); 初期 = initial/opening stage (the very start); 初 = the beginning point itself; 阶段 = one phase in a development sequence; 时期 = a broader era or epoch
末 = end/last in general (literary); 月底 = the end of the current month (time marker); 期末 = end-of-term (educational context only)
华人 = any ethnic Chinese person (worldwide); 华裔 = person of Chinese descent born/raised abroad; 华侨 = Chinese national residing overseas (retains PRC/ROC citizenship)
扩展 = broaden scope or function (neutral); 扩张 = aggressive territorial/political expansion; 扩充 = expand by adding members or items; 扩大 = increase in scale or size
积极性 = enthusiasm as an abstract capacity or quality (formal); 干劲 = work drive, practical zeal (colloquial); 劲头 = vigor or spirit in the moment (colloquial, vivid)
轻易 = carelessly easy; often used in negative 不轻易 ("not lightly/rashly"); 容易 = inherently easy or prone to (neutral, describes nature of thing)
礼 = broad concept: ritual propriety AND gift; 礼节 = specific ceremonial etiquette and rites; 礼貌 = everyday polite manner/courtesy
电灯 = electric lamp specifically (contrasts with oil/candle); 灯 = any lamp or light source
哥 = any elder brother (non-specific); 大哥 = the eldest brother specifically, or respectful address to an older male
例子 = a general illustrative instance or example; 案例 = a documented case study or legal precedent; 榜样 = a role model, a positive example to emulate
途中 = at some point during the journey (locational/temporal mid-way); 一路 = throughout the entire journey, all the way (adverbial, emphasises continuity)
眼 is the morpheme/bound form (used in compounds); 眼睛 is the standalone noun for 'eye(s)' in everyday speech.
All mean 'for example': 比如说 is spoken/emphatic (adds 说); 比如 is casual spoken; 例如 is neutral written; 譬如 is literary/formal.
例子 = neutral instance/example; 案例 = case/precedent (legal or analytical); 典型 = classic/representative example; 榜样 = role model to emulate.
扩展 = extend scope/functionality (neutral); 扩大 = enlarge scale generally; 扩张 = aggressive expansion (territory, power); 扩充 = expand by adding members/content.
极了 is a post-adjective suffix (好极了); 极 is the free adverb (pre-adj, neutral); 极其 is formal/written; 万分 is used only with emotional adjectives.
迷 is the general 'fan/enthusiast' (used as suffix or standalone); 歌迷 = music/singer fan specifically; 球迷 = sports fan specifically.
胖 = fat/plump (of people); 肥 = fat (of animals, or clothes that are loose/baggy); using 肥 for a person is blunt/pejorative.
外语 = foreign language (general, spoken or written); 外文 = foreign language in its written/textual form (used for books, documents, written materials).
名牌儿 is the 儿化 colloquial/Beijing spoken variant of 名牌; same meaning but 名牌 is the standard written/formal form used in most contexts.
美食 = fine food/cuisine (everyday word, also used as a category: 美食文化); 佳肴 = delicious dishes (literary/formal, used in toasts or written descriptions).
一流 = top-ranked, first-rate (comparative rank: 一流大学); 优良 = excellent in quality/character (traits, breeds, traditions — not a rank comparison).
水灾 = flood disaster (the event/calamity, emphasis on damage); 洪水 = floodwater/deluge (the water itself, also used figuratively).
脚步 = footstep (concrete, audible — the sound or act of steps); 步伐 = pace/gait (manner and rhythm of walking, more abstract/formal).
情感 = emotion/feeling (affective, emotional bond — 情感上的联系); 感觉 = feeling/sensation (perception via senses or gut impression — more neutral/cognitive).
厂长 = factory director (industrial plant head); 主任 = department/office director/head (institutional); 经理 = manager/director (commercial company).
从中 = from within a situation (source of action: 从中学习); 其中 = among them (anaphoric reference to a set); 当中 = in the middle/among (spatial or centered focus).
高速公路 = expressway (full formal name, the road itself); 高速 alone = high-speed (adj) or informal short for 高速公路 — also means speed level, not just a road.
女孩/女孩儿 = girl any age (neutral); 女生 = female student/schoolgirl context; 少女 = teenage/maiden (literary); 姑娘 = warm colloquial young woman; 女朋友 = romantic girlfriend only
气体 = gas as a physical state (science); 天然气 = natural gas (utility); 煤气 = coal gas / domestic gas (cooking/heating); 柴油 = diesel fuel (vehicles); 燃料 = fuel in general (any type)

好玩儿 = fun/amusing (colloquial, about an activity); 有趣 = interesting/amusing (neutral adj); 乐趣 = joy/delight (noun, inner feeling); 趣味 = personal taste/liking or charm (noun); 娱乐 = entertainment/leisure (noun/verb, broader)
运气 = luck (good or bad, neutral); 好运 = good luck (simply positive); 福 = blessing/fortune (cultural, ceremonial); 福气 = blessed lot/good fortune (earned or destined, warm)
渐渐 = gradually (natural, often imperceptible change); 逐渐 = gradually (more formal, measurable process); 逐步 = step by step (deliberate, planned stages)

货 = goods/cargo (general, incl. freight); 百货 = general merchandise (retail variety); 商品 = commodity (market economics term); 产品 = product (manufactured output); 物资 = supplies/materials (logistics, often strategic)

拿到 = get/grab (physical, hands-on); 得到 = get/receive (general, neutral); 获得 = obtain/gain (formal, achievements/awards); 取得 = achieve/obtain (formal, results/qualifications); 获 = obtain/capture (formal morpheme, written style)
男子 = man/male (formal noun, e.g. in 男子组); 男士 = gentleman/man (polite address, service contexts); 绅士 = gentleman (implies refined conduct, Western overtone)
走开 = go away / move aside (imperative, abrupt, short-range); 离开 = leave/depart (neutral, from a place or person, any distance)
其中 = among them / from them (anaphoric reference, pointing back); 从中 = from within that / therefrom (extracting benefit or information from a situation)
今后 = from now on (time adverb, forward-looking); 未来 = the future (general noun/adj); 前途 = personal prospects/career future (evaluative, about a person's potential)
宝石 = gemstone (physical object); 宝贵 = valuable/precious (abstract: time, experience); 珍贵 = precious/rare (of objects or moments); 珍稀 = rare and precious (emphasis on scarcity, of species/resources)

分数 = score/grade (numeric, exam result); 等级 = rank/grade (hierarchical tier, formal); 级别 = level/rank (position in a system or organization); 档次 = quality tier/class (consumer or social grade, colloquial)
进去 = go in / enter (directional complement, spoken, toward speaker's perspective); 进入 = enter/go into (formal verb, used in writing and fixed phrases)
好看 = good-looking / nice (general, any gender, also means 'enjoyable to watch'); 英俊 = handsome (specifically of men, more literary/formal)
大门 = main gate / grand entrance (physical structure, often imposing); 入口 = entrance/entry (functional point of access, also used abstractly)
货 = physical goods in transit/trade (cargo emphasis); 商品 = commodity as market item; 产品 = manufactured product; 物资 = supplies/material resources (logistics/state context)
渐渐 = gradual natural drift (spoken, neutral); 逐渐 = gradually, slightly more written/formal; 逐步 = step by step (deliberate, planned progression)
分数 = numeric score or fraction; 级别 = hierarchical rank (administrative); 等级 = rank/grade in a fixed scale; 档次 = quality tier (consumer/market register)
厅 = hall or living room (general); 大厅 = large public lobby/hall (e.g. hotel, station); 客厅 = living room specifically in a home
身高 = measured height (formal, e.g. medical records); 个子 = colloquial stature (general spoken); 个儿 = colloquial stature (northern/Beijing dialect variant)
坏处 = noun: a downside/drawback; 有害 = adjective: harmful/damaging; 危害 = verb/noun: endanger, pose a threat (systemic); 伤害 = verb: to hurt/injure (person, feelings)
群体 = sociological group/community (formal, written); 伙 = colloquial bunch/gang or partner (informal); 团 = a lump/group (also classifier for lumps/troops)
伙 = colloquial group or gang-mate (informal, can be pejorative); 伙伴 = companion/partner (social, friendship); 伴侣 = romantic/life partner (intimate relationship)

好看 = good-looking (anything: people, objects, scenery); 帅 = handsome/cool (adjective, colloquial praise); 帅哥 = handsome guy (vocative address term, colloquial); 英俊 = handsome (formal adjective, men only)
内心 = one's inner self/depths (literary, formal introspection); 心里 = in one's heart/mind right now (colloquial, situational feeling)
贺卡 = greeting/congratulations card (holidays, birthdays); 请帖 = invitation card (traditional style); 请柬 = formal invitation card (official/written); 名片 = business card (professional identity)
地面 = the ground/floor (physical underfoot surface); 面 = any face or surface (general, also spatial classifier); 平面 = flat plane (geometric/technical term)
毕业 = to graduate (the act); 毕业生 = graduate (person who has finished any degree); 研究生 = graduate student (currently enrolled in postgraduate study)

首 = head/chief (literary, also classifier for songs/poems); 组长 = small team/group leader; 所长 = head of an institute or bureau; 领导 = everyday boss/leader (also verb: to lead); 领导人 = official leader (formal title); 领袖 = top political figure/chief (elevated, reverential)
灰 = ash; gray (basic adjective, also means ash/dust); 灰色 = gray (color noun compound, more specific to the color itself; also used metaphorically for ambiguity)
绿 = green (basic color adjective); 绿色 = green (color noun; also metaphor for eco-friendly); 青 = blue-green (traditional color term, also means young/unripe)
头 is neutral 'head' (body part, also top/beginning); 脑袋 is colloquial/informal; 首 is a classifier for songs/poems or literary 'chief/head' — not the body part
身高 is the formal measurement of a person's height; 个子 is the everyday spoken word for stature; 个儿 is colloquial northern dialect for size/height — all near-synonymous but differ in register
内心 is a noun meaning 'inner self / soul' (often used as subject); 心里 is a locative phrase meaning 'in one's heart/mind' (used adverbially)
旅馆 is a generic/older word for inn or hotel; 宾馆 is a standard/state-run hotel; 酒店 is modern, often upscale — same concept but grading in formality and class
房屋 is formal 'building/house'; 屋 is literary/short for room or house; 屋子 is colloquial 'room'; 住房 emphasises residential function/housing as a social concept; 公寓 specifically means apartment building
老家 is colloquial 'native hometown' (where family roots are); 家乡 is the everyday spoken word for hometown; 故乡 is literary/formal for native place
丈夫 is the neutral/formal word for husband; 老公 is colloquial and affectionate — same referent, different register
前 is the base morpheme (front/before); 前面 and 前头 are synonymous locatives (ahead/in front), 前头 being slightly more colloquial; 面前 means 'in front of / before (someone)', always person-referential
事实上 stresses known facts ('the fact is...'); 实际上 stresses practical/real-world reality; 其实 is conversational and corrects an assumption ('actually...')

快点儿 is an exclamatory urge ('hurry up!', often directed at others); 赶快 is an adverb meaning 'quickly, hurry' (neutral volition); 赶紧 emphasises self-motivated promptness to avoid missing an opportunity
多久 is a question word asking 'how long?'; 久 is a plain adjective/adverb meaning 'long (time)' used in statements
灰心 means 'to lose heart, be discouraged' (internal emotional state); 泄气 means 'to lose steam, be deflated' — more vivid, also used for literal deflating
盼望 is 'to long for, hope for' with patient expectation; 渴望 is stronger — 'to crave, yearn for' with urgent desire
公路 is a public road/highway (general); 道路 is formal 'road/path' (also figurative); 高速 specifically means expressway/motorway (also 'high speed')
闲 is an adjective 'idle, free (not busy)'; 空闲 is a noun/adj for free time/spare time; 休闲 specifically means leisure, relaxation as an activity
高级 means high-grade or senior (in rank, quality, or level); 高档 is specifically commercial — premium/upscale (price tier, goods), not used for rank
超 is a prefix/verb meaning 'exceed a threshold' or 'super-'; 超过 is a verb 'to exceed, surpass' (quantity or performance); 高于 means 'higher than' and is specifically comparative for levels/values
诚信 is an internal quality — honesty and integrity as character; 名誉 is one's external reputation / good name as perceived by others
请帖 is an invitation card in traditional/folk contexts (weddings, celebrations); 请柬 is a formal/official invitation card — same concept but 请柬 is more formal
小声 is a manner adverb 'in a low voice'; 声音 is 'sound/voice' as a physical phenomenon; 嗓子 is the physical throat/vocal organ; 口气 is the tone or attitude conveyed by speech
All mean 'actually/in fact'. 其实 corrects a mistaken assumption (conversational pivot); 实际上 grounds in concrete reality; 事实上 appeals to established fact ('the fact is...').

All relate to 'in front'. 前 is the bare morpheme; 前面 is neutral 'in front (of sth)'; 前头 is colloquial spoken; 面前 is 'before sb' (relational, often figurative); 跟前 is 'right beside/in front of sb' (very close proximity).
All mean 'inside/interior'. 内 is a formal/written morpheme (内部, 国内); 里面 is everyday spoken (physical space); 里头 is colloquial/northern spoken.
All involve sky/air. 空中 = mid-air/airspace (where planes fly); 天上 = up in the sky/heavens (celestial, poetic); 天空 = the sky as a visual expanse (noun); 太空 = outer space (beyond atmosphere).
All dismiss a concern. 没事儿 is colloquial Beijing-dialect (erhua); 没什么 is neutral ('it's nothing / not much'); 不要紧 is polite reassurance ('it doesn't matter'), slightly more formal.

All mean 'one after another'. 先后 stresses ordered sequence; 陆续 stresses a gradual continuous stream; 接连 stresses unbroken succession (no gap); 纷纷 stresses many acting simultaneously/in a rush (en masse).

All relate to 'kind/type'. 类 is the abstract class/category (morpheme); 种类 = the variety/range of types (diverse spread); 类型 = a specific classified type; 品种 = breed or commercial variety (biological/product).
Both mean kilogram. 公斤 is the everyday spoken form; 千克 is the official SI/scientific term, used in technical and written contexts.
All mean 'close/intimate'. 亲密 = emotional intimacy between people; 密切 = close (of formal relations, connections, cooperation — not personal warmth); 亲热 = warm and affectionate in manner/behavior.
Both mean 'in one's heart/mind'. 心里 is everyday spoken; 心中 is literary/formal, often used in written or elevated registers.
All express 'seems like'. 看上去 is specifically visual ('looks like', based on sight); 好像 is colloquial general resemblance; 似乎 is literary/tentative ('it would appear').

All involve strength/force. 强度 = measurable intensity/degree (of effort, light, exercise — not personal); 力量 = strength/power as capacity or force (abstract, can be moral); 力气 = physical bodily strength (colloquial, effort of muscles).
Both name secondary-school institutions. 中学 is the general term covering junior and senior high; 初中 is specifically junior high (grades 7–9), the first three years of 中学.
Both translate as 'insect'. 昆虫 is the scientific/formal term (true insects only); 虫子 is colloquial and broader — covers bugs, worms, caterpillars, and any crawling creature.
Both involve letters. 信 = letter in general (also: to trust); 来信 = specifically an incoming/received letter (directional: 来 = coming in).
Exact synonyms for kilogram; 公斤 is everyday colloquial, 千克 is the formal SI term used in science/official writing.
All express 'already/long since'; 早就 stresses the speaker's emphasis (often mild exasperation), 早已 is slightly more literary/neutral, 已 is a literary particle seldom used alone in speech.
Both mean 'since (a past time)'; 自从 introduces the start point as a clause or noun phrase and is more colloquial, 以来 follows a time noun/phrase and is more formal/written.
Both translate 'finally/at last'; 终于 marks a long-awaited outcome (neutral relief), 总算 carries the speaker's sense of effort finally paying off (more emotional/colloquial).

All express 'type/kind/variety'; 种类 = range of categories (countable set); 类 = broad class/genus; 类型 = formal typed classification; 样式 = visible style or pattern; 品种 = variety/breed (biological or product).
All cluster around 'large' but differ: 大规模 = large in scope/scale (of operations or projects); 大型 = large in physical size or category; 大量 = large in quantity/amount.
All relate to 'light'; 光 = light in general or 'only/merely'; 灯 = lamp/fixture; 光线 = ray or quality of (natural) light; 灯光 = artificial lighting or stage light.
All express comparison 'like/as'; 像 is the common verb 'to resemble'; 如同 and 犹如 are more literary equivalents used in written or formal comparisons, with 犹如 slightly more poetic.
All express 'seems/appears'; 好像 is colloquial and can also mean 'like'; 似乎 is more tentative and written; 看起来 is literal 'looking at it, it seems' — visual-based inference.
Both involve lateness; 迟 is an adjective/adverb ('late; slow') used attributively or in compounds, 迟到 is a resultative verb meaning 'to arrive late' and takes a subject.
All describe loudness; 响 = to make sound / be loud (verb/adj, broad); 响亮 = loud and clear (evaluative adj, positive); 大声 = loudly, in a loud voice (manner adverb, neutral).
Both mean 'to listen'; 收听 = tune in to / listen to (radio or broadcast, receiver-oriented); 倾听 = listen attentively to a person (deferential, relational).
Both mean to make a phone call; 打电话 is the everyday general expression; 拨打 is more formal/written (used in announcements, instructions: 'please dial…').
大都 is a manner adverb (generally, for the most part — literary/formal); 大多 is a quantifier-adverb (most of — focuses on proportion of a set, more colloquial)
男人 = everyday neutral 'man'; 男子 = formal/literary noun (sports, legal); 男性 = gender-category term (demographic/scientific); 男 = bound prefix in compounds
会员 = paid/club member (subscription or dues-based); 成员 = member of any group or team (neutral); 委员 = committee member (official role)
夫妻 = everyday word for a couple (husband and wife, informal/neutral); 夫妇 = slightly more formal/written register
意思 = the meaning/sense of words or a signal; 想法 = one's personal viewpoint or thought; 主意 = a concrete plan or idea (actionable); 大意 = the gist or main point of a text
可能 = possible/maybe (also adj and noun: 'possibility'); 也许 = maybe (pure adverb, colloquial); 说不定 = maybe/who knows (implies genuine uncertainty, idiomatic)
摩托车 is the standard full word for motorcycle; 摩托 is the abbreviated colloquial form, also used in compounds (摩托艇 etc.)
猴子 is the everyday standalone word for monkey; 猴 is the morpheme/character used in compounds and as the zodiac animal name
型 = type/model as a bound suffix in compounds; 型号 = the full product model/spec designation (number + type, e.g. iPhone型号)
人力 = human manpower/capacity (resource planning); 人工 = manual/human-operated (as adj: artificial; or noun: manual labor cost); 劳动 = labor as physical toil (ideological/economic sense)
药物 = a drug or medication (the substance); 医学 = medicine as a scientific field of study — both translate to 'medicine' in English
小姐 = Miss/young lady (unmarried or customer-service address); 太太 = Mrs./wife (informal respectful); 夫人 = Mrs./wife (formal/honorific, official contexts)
区 = administrative district (bound morpheme); 地区 = broader geographic region/area; 山区 = mountain area specifically (topographic subtype)
大都 is adverb only (mostly do X); 大多 can also precede a noun as a determiner (most of the X). 大多 is slightly more formal/written.
相互 is adverb (modifies a verb: 相互帮助); 彼此 is a pronoun (subject/object slot: 彼此了解); 相 is a literary verb-prefix, not a free adverb.
大自然 is noun only (the natural world); 自然 is both noun and adverb (naturally, of course). Use 大自然 when emphasizing nature as a whole entity.
嘴巴 is colloquial (everyday speech); 嘴 is the neutral general form and extends to spouts/nozzles; 口腔 is anatomical/medical (oral cavity).
All mean 'not necessarily'. 未必 is written/formal; 不见得 is colloquial spoken; 不一定 is neutral, usable in both registers.
All mean 'no need to'. 不必 is written/formal; 用不着 is colloquial neutral; 甭 is informal Beijing spoken dialect.
不曾 stresses experiential negation (have never done); 未 is a literary/written particle meaning 'not yet' with no such experiential nuance.
接下来 = 'what comes next' in a sequence (neutral); 接着 = immediately following in time (temporal continuity); 其次 = 'secondly' in an enumeration (discourse listing).
Both mean 'not only'. 不光 is colloquial spoken; 不但 is neutral/written. Both pair with 还/也/而且 in the second clause.
All mean 'no matter (what/how)'. 不管 is colloquial; 无论 is formal/written; 不论 is intermediate, slightly more formal than 不管.
北 is the basic direction word; 北方 denotes 'the north' as a broad cultural/geographic region; 北部 is 'the northern part of' a specific entity (country, city).
目前 = at present, the current moment (point in time); 近期 = the near term, a span of time close to now (can be past or future).
新年 = New Year in general, often the Lunar New Year; 元旦 = specifically January 1st (solar calendar); 除夕 = New Year's Eve (the night before).
国庆节 is the full name of the PRC National Day holiday; 国庆 is the short form used attributively (国庆假期) or in contexts where 节 is understood.
Same food; 面条儿 is the Beijing-dialect erhua variant, more colloquial and regional; 面条 is the standard Mandarin form used in writing and across regions.
在乎 = to care/mind (affirmative); 不在乎 = not to care — learners may treat them as interchangeable synonyms rather than a negation pair; each must be used with the correct polarity
Both mean 'not very', but 不大 also carries 'not often/rarely' (frequency sense); 不太 is neutral degree attenuation only
Same meaning; 姐 is the short bound form used in compounds (e.g. 大姐) or addressing; 姐姐 is the freestanding spoken form for 'older sister'
All mean 'outside': 外 is a bound morpheme/prefix; 外边 (colloq.) and 外面 (neutral) are interchangeable locatives; 之外 is a formal/written postposition meaning 'apart from/beyond'
Paired connectives: 一方面…另一方面 = 'on the one hand…on the other hand'; learners confuse which clause each introduces or omit the counterpart

All restrict scope to 'merely/only': 仅/仅仅 are neutral restrictors; 只不过 and 只是 add a mild dismissive/concessive shade; 无非 = 'nothing but' (slightly emphatic); 不过 doubles as a concessive connector ('however')
老人 = old person (neutral, general); 老头儿 = old man (colloquial, slightly familiar); 老年 = old age (the life stage/period, not a person label); 年纪 = age in years (often of elderly, colloquial)
痛 = physical pain/ache (neutral); 疼 = physical ache, often with tenderness or affection ('dote on'); 痛苦 = suffering/anguish (emotional or profound physical — a noun/adj)
Both mean 'outline/synopsis': 大纲 is an official or curricular syllabus (broader scope); 提纲 is a working outline or notes-skeleton for a speech/essay (personal use)
Both introduce a cause: 由于 = 'due to' (neutral, factual cause, can precede or follow the result clause); 出于 = 'out of' a motive or feeling (always introduces internal motivation, e.g. 出于好意)

All relate to 'original': 原 = attributive 'former/original' (literary); 原有 = 'pre-existing, what was already there' (factual); 原始 = 'primitive/primordial' or 'raw/source' (strongest sense of going back to origins)

总体 = overall/in aggregate (scope adverb or adj, e.g. 总体来看); 全局 = the whole situation considered as one unit (often strategic); 格局 = the underlying pattern/configuration of a situation (structural, big-picture framing)

All mean 'other': 别的 = colloquial 'other/else' (can modify both people and things); 其他 = neutral 'other' (people or things, standard written form); 其它 = variant of 其他 restricted to non-person referents
开业 = to open a business for the first time (event, inauguration); 营业 = to be in business/operation (ongoing state, e.g. 营业时间 = business hours)
Both mean 'over there': 那里 = neutral 'that place/there'; 那边 = 'that side/direction over there' (emphasises spatial direction or side rather than just location)
Both mean 'now': 今 is literary/formal (used in set phrases 今日, 今年, or classical prose); 此时 = 'at this moment/right now' (narrative temporal marker, more vivid but still written-register)
局 = bureau/office OR a round/game (classifier); 全局 = the overall situation as a whole; 局势 = the (political/strategic) situation at a given moment — all share the 局 root but differ sharply in meaning
笔记 = notes (the written content of what one records); 本子 = notebook (the physical object); learners confuse them when translating 'take notes in a notebook'
痛 = physical pain/ache (neutral); 疼 = physical ache + colloquial 'dote on'; 痛苦 = emotional anguish/suffering (not bodily)
处罚 = penalize (administrative, fines); 惩罚 = punish (moral/disciplinary); 制裁 = sanctions (political/legal, formal register)
病人 = sick person/patient (everyday spoken); 患者 = patient (formal medical/written context)
诗 = poem or poetry (single work or the art); 诗歌 = poetry as a genre/collective term (slightly more formal)
毒 = poison/venom (noun/morpheme); 有毒 = poisonous/toxic (predicate adjective); 毒品 = narcotics/illegal drugs (law register)
体力 = physical stamina/endurance; 力气 = brute physical effort (colloquial); 力量 = strength/force (abstract or collective); 有力 = forceful/powerful (attributive adjective)
价钱 = price (everyday spoken transaction); 价格 = price (market/listed, formal); 代价 = cost/price (figurative sacrifice); 成本 = production/unit cost (business)
相片 = photograph (physical/personal print); 图片 = image/picture (digital or generic); 画面 = visual frame/scene (video, screen, painted composition)
所在 = whereabouts/location of something (abstract, 'where X is'); 地点 = specific concrete place/venue
乘客 = passenger (the rider); 乘务员 = cabin/train attendant (crew serving passengers)
负责人 = the person in charge (noun, a role); 负责 = to be responsible for / to take charge of (verb/adjective)
价钱 = spoken/retail price (colloquial); 价 = bound morpheme in compounds (不等价, 价值); 价格 = formal market/listed price
读音 = pronunciation of a specific character/word (how to read it); 语音 = speech sounds broadly, or voice (tech: voice message); 口音 = regional accent/speech flavor
特性 = intrinsic property, often scientific/technical; 特点 = distinctive feature/trait (neutral, everyday); 性质 = essential nature or character of something
纯 = pure/unmixed (physical substance); 纯洁 = morally pure, innocent (of heart); 单纯 = simple, naive (personality trait)
静 = quiet/still (literary adj, inner calm); 安静 = quiet without disturbance (environment/behavior, everyday); 寂静 = deep silence (literary, atmospheric)
兔 = literary/zodiac form, used in compounds (兔年); 兔子 = everyday colloquial word for rabbit/hare
鼠 = literary/formal form, appears in compounds (鼠疫, 鼠标); 老鼠 = everyday colloquial word for mouse/rat
雨 = rain as a weather phenomenon; 雨水 = rainwater as a substance, or accumulated rainfall (also a solar term)
读书 = read/study broadly (colloquial, often = attend school); 阅读 = read silently (formal, written texts); 朗读 = read aloud expressively; 念 = recite or read aloud (casual)
认得 = recognize/know someone by face (colloquial, personal acquaintance); 辨认 = identify by careful scrutiny; 识别 = identify/distinguish systematically (technical)
餐馆 = restaurant only; 饭店 = restaurant OR hotel (regionally ambiguous); 宾馆 = standard/state hotel; 酒店 = upscale modern hotel
有力 = forceful, effective (of arguments, measures, evidence); 强 = strong/powerful in general; 强大 = powerful and formidable (of entities, nations, forces)
疗养 = recuperate with medical treatment (sanatorium-style); 休养 = recover through rest (no emphasis on treatment)
排名 = ranking/placing (act of ranking or one's rank in a list); 名次 = specific placing in a competition; 级别 = rank level in a hierarchy/system; 等级 = grade or tier in a classification
餐馆 = restaurant only; 饭店 = restaurant OR hotel (casual dual use); 宾馆 = standard/state hotel; 酒店 = upscale modern hotel
右 = directional morpheme/adjective (right); 右边 = noun phrase for the right side as a location
排 = a physical row (seats, troops); 列 = a column/series, also 'to enumerate'; 排列 = to arrange in order (verb)
海 = sea/ocean (generic morpheme); 大海 = the open sea (expressive, vast); 海洋 = the oceans collectively (formal/scientific); 海水 = seawater (the substance, not the body)
季 = season as a morpheme (in compounds: 季度, 四季); 季节 = standalone word for season; 淡季 = off-season (commerce/tourism)
难过 = general feeling of sadness/upset (colloquial); 伤心 = heartbroken, deeply pained; 悲伤 = sorrowful, mournful (more literary/formal)
马路 = paved urban road/avenue; 道路 = road or path (general/formal); 街 = street (the road itself); 街道 = street with admin-unit sense (also: residential district)
环 = ring/loop shape (generic, also a link/component); 戒指 = finger ring (jewelry only)
铃 = the bell/buzzer object; 钟 = wall clock or large bell; 铃声 = the sound of a bell/ringtone (the sound, not the object)
剪子 = scissors (colloquial/dialectal); 剪刀 = scissors (standard written form); same object, register differs
景象 = visual scene/panoramic sight (objective); 情景 = scene charged with emotion (atmosphere of a moment); 场面 = visible occasion/spectacle (scale, social impression)
偷偷 = secretly, concealing intent or action from others; 悄悄 = quietly, without noise — may or may not involve secrecy
碰到 = run into accidentally; 见 = to see/meet (neutral, general); 逢 = happen to meet, on a recurring occasion (literary); 会晤 = formal meeting (diplomatic/official)
转动 = rotate/spin on an axis; 围绕 = revolve around a center, or center a topic on; 绕 = wind around, detour, wrap
偷偷 stresses concealment/deception (like a thief); 悄悄 stresses quietness/silence. 偷偷 can be noisy but hidden; 悄悄 implies near-silence.
签名 = personal autograph or casual signing; 签字 = official signature on a document; 签署 = formally sign (agreement/declaration); 签订 = conclude AND sign (a contract/treaty).
句 is primarily a measure word/classifier for sentences and set phrases; 句子 is the standalone noun 'sentence'. 句 as a noun is literary/abbreviated.

真诚 = general sincerity (everyday); 真挚 = deep heartfelt sincerity (written/emotional); 诚挚 = formal/literary heartfelt; 诚恳 = earnest sincerity in attitude/manner; 衷心 = from the bottom of one's heart (fixed expressions, e.g. 衷心感谢).
短处 = personal weak point, informal and often self-deprecating; 缺点 = general flaw or shortcoming in anything; 弱点 = a vulnerability or soft spot that can be exploited.
慢 = adjective/predicate 'slow'; 慢慢 = adverb 'slowly/gradually' (also implies patience); 迟缓 = slow and sluggish (often negative, of reaction or movement).
慢慢 = slowly, at a relaxed pace (pace focus); 逐渐 = gradually over time (time focus); 逐步 = step by step, in stages (process focus, more formal).
大小 = general size (big-or-small dimension, colloquial); 尺寸 = precise measurements/dimensions (garments, objects); 规模 = scale or magnitude of an organisation, project, or event.
小型 = small-scale (attributive, neutral); 大型 = large-scale (attributive, neutral); 庞大 = huge and unwieldy — implies excess or burden, not merely size.
衬衣 = shirt (colloquial, generic); 衬衫 = shirt/blouse (slightly more formal, can refer to both men's shirts and women's blouses).
肩 = shoulder (morpheme, literary, used in compounds like 双肩); 肩膀 = shoulder (full colloquial noun, used in everyday speech).
鞋 = shoes (general term); 皮鞋 = leather shoes (formal/dress shoes); 球鞋 = sneakers/athletic shoes (sports context).
皮 = skin/peel/leather (broad: body skin, fruit skin, leather material); 皮肤 = skin specifically as a body organ (medical/biological usage).
单一 = single/uniform/homogeneous (adjective describing lack of variety, e.g. 单一结构); 单 = single/odd number/a bill or form (broader: also means receipt, list, unpaired).
慢 is an adjective (or plain adverb); 慢慢 is a reduplication adverb stressing gradual/leisurely pace; 迟缓 (5k) is literary/clinical, implying sluggishness or delay
慢慢 = slowly/at one's own pace (colloquial, physical or abstract); 逐步 (5k) = step-by-step (deliberate stages); 逐渐 (5k) = imperceptibly, little by little (change over time)
战士 = fighter/warrior (heroic or ideological connotation); 兵 = soldier as morpheme or collective troops; 士兵 (5k) = plain common soldier (neutral, rank-and-file)
有的 = some (of a defined set, distributive: 有的人…有的人…); 有一些 = there are some (existential quantity); 有些 (5k) = some/somewhat (also degree adverb before adjectives)
歌 is colloquial/informal and can refer to the act of singing or a song; 歌曲 is the standard formal/written term for a musical composition (song as a piece)
随后 = immediately following in sequence (right after); 不久 = an unspecified short time later (temporal gap, not necessarily immediate)
南方 = the South as a cultural/geographic region (e.g. 南方人); 南部 = the southern portion of a specific territory or country (administrative sub-area)
声 is a bound morpheme/literary unit (also a measure word for sounds); 声音 is the standalone spoken word for sound or voice in everyday use
小型/大型 are neutral attributive adjectives of scale (small-scale / large-scale); 庞大 (5k) adds a connotation of unwieldy bulk or excess
球场 = court or field for ball games; 操场 (5k) = school exercise yard / drill ground; 体育场 = full stadium (spectator venue)
春节 = the Lunar New Year holiday (multi-day festival); 除夕 (5k) = Lunar New Year's Eve (the night before); 元旦 (5k) = January 1st (Gregorian New Year's Day)
职工 = workers/staff of an institution, factory, or state enterprise (collective, official); 员工 (5k) = employees of a company (corporate/modern usage)
土 = soil/earth as a material substance; 土地 (5k) = land as property, territory, or arable ground
平稳 = stable and even (no fluctuation, physical or abstract steadiness); 顺利 (5k) = going smoothly without obstacles or hitches (process succeeds as hoped)
言语 = speech/words as a compound noun (everyday or formal); 言 (5k) = literary bound morpheme for words/speech; 语 (5k) = literary bound morpheme for language/utterance
夏 is a bare morpheme (mostly in compounds); 夏天 is spoken/everyday 'summer'; 夏季 is written/formal 'summer season' — not interchangeable in register.
工人 = blue-collar manual worker; 员工 = collective term for company staff (white- and blue-collar); 职工 = workers and staff of an institution/factory (official/formal, implies employment relationship).
结构 = abstract/analytical structure or composition; 构造 = concrete physical construction or build (geology, machinery). 构造 implies how something is assembled; 结构 how it is organized.
稳 is a monosyllabic adjective ('steady, not wobbling'); 稳定 is a disyllabic adj/noun/verb meaning 'stable; stability; to stabilize' — broader, used in formal and abstract contexts.
步 = a single physical step or pace (also a measure word); 步伐 = gait or collective pace/tempo (figurative: pace of development); 步骤 = procedural step in a sequence (not physical).

站住 = imperative 'halt!/stop!' (physical, directed at a person); 停止 = cease an ongoing action (neutral, intransitive); 阻止 = block/prevent something already underway (transitive); 拦 = physically intercept or block passage.

味道 = flavor/smell (neutral, most common); 味儿 = colloquial/dialectal variant of 味道; 滋味 = taste plus the feeling/aftertaste (can be figurative); 口味 = one's personal taste preference or a food's flavor profile.
学科 = academic discipline or field of study (broad: linguistics, biology); 科目 = specific subject item on a curriculum or examination list (narrower, administrative).
车站 = general term for a bus or train station; 地铁站 = specifically a subway/metro station. 地铁站 is a subset of 车站 but learners confuse them when describing urban transit stops.
盒子 = small box or case (cosmetics, gift); 箱子 = large box or suitcase (general); 行李箱 = specifically a travel suitcase or luggage trunk.
暑假 = summer vacation (fixed school/academic holiday); 休假 = taking personal leave or a holiday (worker context, voluntary, any time of year).
星期日 = Sunday (standard written form); 礼拜天 = Sunday (colloquial, with religious connotation 礼拜 = worship). Same day, different registers.
晴天 = noun phrase 'sunny/clear day'; 晴朗 = adjective 'sunny and cloudless' (describes weather or sky). 晴天 is a time noun; 晴朗 is a predicative/attributive adjective.

会晤 = formal diplomatic meeting (heads of state, official); 会谈 = formal talks (political or high-level, may involve multiple sessions); 洽谈 = negotiate/discuss for business purposes (commercial register).
欺骗 = to deceive (general verb); 骗子 = a swindler/cheat (person noun); 作弊 = to cheat specifically in an exam, game, or competition (situational).
泪 is the literary/bare morpheme (poetry, compounds); 泪水 is more expressive/written (emphasizes the physical liquid); 眼泪 is the neutral everyday word for 'tears' (spoken and written).
味道 = general flavor/smell (neutral); 味儿 = colloquial spoken variant of 味道; 滋味 = taste + inner feeling (often figurative: 心里不是滋味); 口味 = personal taste preference (what one likes).

会谈 = formal talks between parties (political/diplomatic, stated agenda); 洽谈 = business negotiation (commercial context); 会晤 = formal meeting/audience (diplomatic, emphasises the encounter itself, often shorter than 会谈).
老虎 is the common spoken/everyday word for tiger; 虎 is the literary/formal morpheme used in idioms, compounds (虎年, 虎威), and written prose.

小偷 is the neutral everyday word for thief/pickpocket; 小偷儿 is a colloquial Beijing-dialect diminutive of 小偷; 贼 is broader (any criminal/bandit) and also used colloquially as an intensifier (贼厉害 = damn impressive).
地形 = terrain/landforms (types of physical features: hills, plains, rivers — descriptive); 地势 = the lie/configuration of the land (how high/low/sloped it sits — positional).

此刻 = 'this very moment' (literary/emotional, pinpoints an instant); 眼下 = 'right now' (spoken, informal); 目前 = 'at present' (neutral time adverb, written); 当前 = 'current/pressing now' (formal, implies urgency or importance of the present task).

那时 = 'at that time' (anaphoric: refers back to a time just mentioned); 当年 = 'back in those years / in that era' (nostalgic retrospect, specific period); 昔日 = 'in former days' (literary/elevated register, often lyrical).
这里 = 'here / this place' (neutral location); 这边 = 'this side / this way' (directional, often contrasted with 那边; also used to gesture someone over).
高大 = tall and large/imposing (physical size, of people or structures); 挺拔 = tall and erect/straight (elegant upright bearing, literary — trees, figures).
队员 = member of a team or squad (sports, military unit, specific crew); 成员 = member of any group or organization (broader, also used for families, committees, states).

做到 = 'manage to do / succeed in doing' (emphasis on effort overcoming difficulty); 完成 = 'complete / finish' (a task or assignment reaches its end); 实现 = 'realize / bring into being' (a goal, dream, or plan); 取得 = 'obtain / achieve' (results, qualifications, a concrete gain).

赞赏 = admire and praise (both feeling + verbal expression); 欣赏 = enjoy/appreciate aesthetically (art, scenery, talent — internal pleasure); 佩服 = admire someone's skill or character (colloquial, often heartfelt); 羡慕 = envy/admire what someone has (desire to have it yourself).

赞赏 = admire and praise (sincere, can be peer-to-peer); 表扬 = official/public praise (from above, institutional); 夸 = casual verbal praise, also 'exaggerate' (colloquial); 吹捧 = lavish over-the-top praise, flattery (often insincere/sycophantic).
前进 = move forward / advance (intransitive, of people, armies, causes); 推进 = push forward / propel (transitive, someone/something drives a process or object ahead).
票 = ticket (general: transport, event, admission); 票价 = the price of a ticket / fare; 彩票 = lottery ticket (a ticket bought for a chance to win, not for entry).
月亮 = the moon (everyday, poetic, emotional — moonlight poetry); 月球 = the Moon as a celestial/scientific body (technical: lunar missions, astronomy).
粗 = thick/coarse in diameter (also 'rough' in texture or manner); 细 = thin/fine in diameter or detail (also 'meticulous'); 薄 = thin in flat thickness (paper, slices, walls) — not about diameter.

大多数 = the majority (more than half, counting individual items or people — used with countables); 大部分 = most of / a large portion (emphasises share or fraction of a whole — used with mass or abstract quantities too).
民族 = ethnic group / nation / people (general term for any ethnicity or nationality); 中华民族 = the Chinese nation (a specific political-cultural concept encompassing all ethnic groups of China).
大众 = the masses / the general public (neutral to formal, used in media/slogans: 大众文化); 老百姓 = ordinary folks / the common people (colloquial, warm, emphasises everyday citizens).

做到 = manage to do/achieve (a specific result by effort); 完成 = complete/finish a task (focus on finishing); 实现 = realize/fulfil (a dream, goal, vision); 取得 = obtain/acquire (results, qualifications — something gained).

赞赏 = admire + praise (mixed feeling+speech); 欣赏 = appreciate/enjoy aesthetically (no envy, no praise); 佩服 = respect/admire someone's skill (colloquial, internal); 羡慕 = envy/admire (wanting what they have); 表扬 = official praise from above; 夸 = casually praise or exaggerate; 吹捧 = lavish/excessive flattery (pejorative).
靠近 = move physically close to (everyday); 接近 = approach / be close to (physical or abstract, neutral); 靠拢 = close up / draw together (military or group formation).

建成 = complete construction (result, project finished); 建造 = construct/build (physical structure, process); 修建 = build/construct (infrastructure, official tone); 建设 = build and develop (large-scale, incl. society/economy); 建立 = found/establish (an organization, system, or relationship).
打破 = shatter/smash (also break records or limits); 破 = broken state or the act of breaking (broad); 断 = snap/cut off (linear things: rope, signal, contact).
违规 = break a rule/regulation (procedural, administrative); 违反 = violate a law, rule, or principle (broader, incl. laws); 侵犯 = infringe/encroach (rights, territory — implies harming another).
播出 = to air/broadcast (a specific program, one-time output); 广播 = radio broadcasting as medium, or to broadcast generally (also a noun for radio).

挑选 = pick by personal preference (everyday shopping, choosing); 筛选 = screen/filter by criteria (systematic, analytical); 拣 = pick out by hand/visually (physical, informal); 选拔 = select the best candidates (competitive, talent-focused); 选择 = choose from options (general, neutral).
叫作 = be called/known as (everyday, informal naming); 称为 = be called/designated (neutral to formal, written); 称 = call or name (also means 'to weigh'; broader literary usage).
避 = avoid/evade (deliberate effort to stay away from sth); 逃 = flee/escape (physical movement away from danger).
加以 = apply an action to sth (followed by a verb: 加以解释, 加以改进); 予以 = grant/give formally (official contexts: 予以表彰); 给予 = give/render (literary/formal, broader objects: attention, support).
怨 = resent/blame (internal feeling, often directed at fate/others); 指责 = criticize/blame publicly (formal, accusatory); 责怪 = reproach informally (interpersonal, casual register).
登山 = mountain climbing (sport/activity as a whole); 登 = ascend/climb (general, incl. stairs, stages); 攀登 = scale/climb (effort-intensive, figurative too: 攀登高峰).
败 = be defeated (war, competition — formal/literary); 失败 = fail/suffer defeat (general: project, attempt, battle); 输 = lose a contest/game (colloquial, zero-sum, also 'input').
收费 = charge a fee (the act of collecting payment); 免费 = free of charge (everyday, consumer context); 无偿 = free/without compensation (formal/legal, e.g. 无偿援助).

为 = be/act as (literary/formal copula or role); 成为 = become (take on a new status/role, lasting change); 充当 = serve as / fill the role of (temporary, functional); 变成 = turn into (physical/qualitative transformation).
关上 = close/shut (physical objects: door, window; also turn off); 闭幕 = close/end an event (ceremony, conference, exhibition — formal).
前进 = move/march forward (physical or metaphorical progress); 推进 = drive/advance sth forward (active effort, project or policy); 飞跃 = leap/make a great advance (dramatic, non-linear progress).
烧 = burn / cook by heat / have a fever (broad, colloquial); 烘 = bake/dry using indirect heat (oven, fire nearby); 煮 = boil/cook by simmering in liquid.
在场 = be present at an event/scene (often legal/official: witness present); 处在 = be situated in a state or condition (abstract or physical location in a state).
收购 = buy up in bulk / acquire (a company or commodities at scale); no 购买 in this window — group limited to 收购 alone, skip.

All involve movement toward the speaker. 进 = enter (neutral). 进来 = come in (into enclosed space, toward speaker). 上来 = come up (upward toward speaker). 传来 = reach the speaker (sound/news only, not physical). 回来 = come back (returning).
含 = hold in mouth/contain (physical or abstract, single character). 含有 = contain (specific substance/element, formal). 包含 = contain/include (broader set membership, formal).
分为 = formally classified as (describes result category). 分成 = split into (concrete partitioning, focus on outcome parts). 划分 = demarcate/divide (official boundaries or categories).

做梦 = have a dream (while sleeping) or daydream (general). 梦见 = dream of sth specific (see it in a dream). 梦想 = cherished aspiration, to dream of (waking goal). 幻想 = fantasy/daydream (unrealistic imagination).
赢 = win (general, colloquial). 打败 = beat/defeat (an opponent, often physical contest). 战胜 = overcome/prevail over (battle or hardship, more formal/literary).

防 = defend/prevent (ward off threats, proactive). 保护 = protect (general safeguarding). 守护 = guard/watch over (ongoing devoted protection). 爱护 = care for (out of affection, not threat-based). 掩护 = cover/shield (military or covert protection). 预防 = prevent (health/risk precaution before harm). 避免 = avoid (steer clear of sth undesirable).

抄 = copy by hand / plagiarize. 抄写 = copy out by hand (deliberate transcription, study context). 复印 = photocopy (machine reproduction of document). 复制 = duplicate/replicate (digital or physical, broader).
拆 = dismantle/tear down (single action, physical). 拆除 = demolish/remove (official, often structural, typically permanent).
相比 = compare (general, often in 相比之下 'by comparison'). 对比 = contrast (highlight differences, includes noun 'contrast'). 对照 = compare side-by-side (check against, verify).
编 = weave/compile/make up (broad: stories, baskets, numbers). 编织 = weave/knit (physical interlacing of threads/strands). 编辑 = edit/revise (text, media); also 'editor' (person).
下降 = drop/decline (measurable fall in quantity or level). 衰退 = decline/recession (gradual deterioration, economic or physical decay).

登录 = log in (digital, account access). 登陆 = land/come ashore (military or physical landing). 登记 = check in/register (administrative, e.g. hotel check-in). 注册 = register/enroll (formal official registration, one-time).

估计 = estimate/reckon (rough calculation or personal judgment). 预计 = forecast/expect (planned projection, often official). 预测 = predict (analytical, data-based). 预报 = forecast (broadcast prediction, esp. weather).
增大 = increase in size or amount (general growth). 放大 = magnify/enlarge (optical or signal amplification). 扩大 = expand/broaden (scope, scale, influence).

忍 = endure by holding back (suppress reaction). 忍耐 = endure with sustained self-restraint. 承受 = withstand (bear a load or blow). 熬 = tough it out slowly (simmer through difficulty). 挨 = endure passively (stall or suffer through). 吃苦 = endure hardship (accept a hard life).
使劲 and 使劲儿 are near-identical in meaning (exert force/effort); 使劲儿 is the Beijing erhua colloquial form. Learners may treat them as different words — they are stylistic variants.

发出 = emit/send out (sound, light, signals, notifications). 排放 = discharge/emit (pollutants, gases, industrial waste). 放射 = radiate (radiation, rays, spreading outward from a point). 冒 = emit/give off (smoke, steam, bubble up; also 'brave').
客人 = guest or visitor (social context; also general customer). 顾客 = customer/shopper (retail, commercial transaction). 客户 = client/account holder (business relationship, repeat/contracted).

摔 = fall/smash (fall with impact or throw down). 摔倒 = fall down (trip and land, bodily fall). 倒 = topple/fall over (object or person collapsing sideways). 掉 = fall off/drop (detach and drop, often implies separation).
舞 = dance (verb) / wield/brandish (verb); colloquial. 舞蹈 = dance (noun, art form / choreography); more formal.
抽奖 = draw a prize / raffle (the act of drawing). 彩票 = lottery ticket (the physical ticket). Often confused as both relate to lottery/prize contexts.
关上 = close/turn off (physical object: door, window, device). 闭幕 = close (an event/ceremony formally ends; curtain falls).
使劲 = exert physical force or effort (push hard in the moment). 付出 = expend/invest (effort, time, emotion — abstract commitment over time).
逃 = base verb (flee/escape); 逃跑 = flee by running (emphasises running motion); 逃走 = escape and leave (directional completion, not necessarily running)
流 = flow/stream (simple verb, tears, water, time); 流动 = flow/circulate (systemic ongoing movement — crowds, capital, liquids in systems)
长大 = grow up (humans reaching adulthood); 成长 = grow/mature (personal development, also broad); 生长 = grow (biological — plants, organisms); 栽培 = cultivate (plants) or mentor (people, formal)
生成 = generate/produce (technical/computational output); 形成 = form/take shape (gradual structural emergence); 产生 = arise/generate (cause-effect, broad — results, emotions, problems)
递给 = pass to (physical hand-to-hand, no ownership transfer); 交给 = hand over (transfer responsibility, duty, or ownership)

害 = harm (basic, colloquial, can also be noun); 伤害 = hurt/injure (personal, bodily or emotional); 损害 = damage/harm (formal, abstract — interests, health, reputation); 损坏 = damage (physical objects); 危害 = endanger (serious systemic threat); 破坏 = destroy/sabotage (deliberate structural damage)

带有 = carry (a slight quality/tinge — partial, incidental); 有着 = have/possess (literary, emphatic state); 拥有 = own/possess (ongoing state, valuables, rights); 占有 = possess/occupy (with control, sometimes forcibly)
蒙 = bluff/guess blindly (colloquial, no basis); 猜 = guess (everyday, intuitive); 猜测 = guess/conjecture (more considered, some basis); 推测 = surmise/infer (reasoned, evidence-based deduction)
飞 = fly (everyday verb); 飞行 = fly/flight (formal technical, aviation context); 航行 = navigate/sail or fly (sea or air voyage); 航空 = aviation (noun/adj, industry term)

聚 = gather/assemble (people or things, naturally); 集合 = assemble (organised, commanded); 采集 = collect/harvest (specimens, data, natural resources); 收集 = gather/collect (items systematically); 收藏 = collect/keep (for pleasure or preservation — hobby)

掉 = drop/fall off (detach from a surface); 倒 = topple over/collapse (lose upright position); 摔 = fall hard/smash (also throw violently); 摔倒 = fall down (stumble and hit the ground — resultative compound)
Both mean 'to hear'; 听见 = hear (emphasises the sensory event — sound reached you); 听到 = hear (neutral completion — you received the sound)

付出 = expend/invest (effort, money, emotion — what you give); 使劲/使劲儿 = exert force (physical effort, push hard; 儿 is spoken Beijing variant); 拼命 = with all one's might (desperate, intense manner adverb); 全力以赴 = go all out (idiom, total commitment)

出国 = go abroad (verb-action, crossing the border); 国外 = abroad/overseas (location noun, neutral); 海外 = overseas (location noun, implies sea crossing, often diaspora/global); 留学 = study abroad (verb, specifically for education)
害 is the general root (harm as verb/noun); 危害 stresses endangering/threatening (health, society); 伤害 means physically or emotionally hurt (a person).
带有 = carry (a tinge/trait, often negative nuance); 有着 = literary 'have/possess' (stable quality); 拥有 = own/possess (state, often assets); 占有 = possess/occupy (implies control or claim).

Both mean 'to hear (with result)'; 听见 highlights that the sound reached one's awareness; 听到 is interchangeable in most contexts but slightly more common in written usage. Learners confuse them as synonyms.

赶 = hurry/catch (general verb); 赶到 = arrive by hurrying (resultative); 赶快 = imperative adverb 'hurry up!'; 赶紧 = hurry (self-motivated, softer than 赶快); 催 = to rush someone else; 匆忙 = in a rush (manner adjective/adverb).

增 = root morpheme (increase/add); 增多 = grow in number (intransitive, count nouns); 增加 = increase (transitive or intransitive, most neutral); 增长 = grow (often GDP/population, with scale sense); 增添 = add to/enrich (positive connotation); 递增 = increase incrementally/steadily.
介绍 = introduce a person, concept, or product to an audience; 引进 = bring in/import from outside (technology, talent, foreign goods).
推行 = roll out/implement (pushing adoption, often policy); 实行 = put into practice (execute an already-decided plan). 推行 implies active promotion; 实行 implies execution.
跳 = general jump/hop; 蹦 = bounce/spring (short, lively hops); 跳跃 = leap (formal/literary, often figurative).
爱 = love/like (broad, deep); 喜爱 = like/be fond of (preference, lighter); 热爱 = love ardently (a cause, career, country); 恋爱 = be romantically in love (relationship state).
降 = descend/lower (root); 降低 = reduce a level or quantity (transitive: lower costs); 降落 = physically land (aircraft, object); 降临 = befall/descend upon (events, literary).

遇 = encounter (root, often unplanned); 遇见 = run into (colloquial, neutral); 碰见 = bump into (casual, chance); 逢 = meet/on the occasion of (literary/set phrases); 会晤 = meet formally (diplomatic/official).
连接 = connect/link (general, physical or abstract); 衔接 = link up smoothly (join segments without gap); 链接 = hyperlink (tech/internet context).
吸 = inhale/suck in (physical action); 吸收 = absorb/assimilate (physics, nutrients, ideas); 吸取 = draw/extract (lessons, experience); 摄取 = intake/ingest (nutritional/biological).
减轻 = reduce the weight/burden/severity (measurable decrease); 缓解 = ease/relieve temporarily (symptoms, pressure); 缓和 = relax/defuse (tension, conflict, relations).
笑 = laugh or smile broadly (covers both laughter and smiling); 微笑 = smile gently (no laughing, often warm/polite).

补 = patch/mend or make up for (also figurative:补缺); 修 = repair (colloquial, general); 修理 = fix a specific broken object (hands-on); 修复 = restore damaged or destroyed items (includes digital/ecological).
测 = measure/test (root morpheme, often in compounds); 测量 = measure with instruments (surveying, scientific measurement).
查询 = query facts/data/records (look something up); 咨询 = consult an expert or service for advice.
松 = loose/slack (state adjective, or verb: loosen); 放松 = relax/let go (deliberate action, often mental relaxation).
闹 = make noise/fuss/cause trouble (includes rowdy behavior); 吵 = quarrel or be noisy (implies conflict or grating noise); 嘈杂 = clamorous/noisy (adjective, ambient sound, no conflict).
敲门 = knock on a door (fixed phrase); 敲 = knock/rap (deliberate strike on a surface); 磕 = accidentally knock/tap against something.
缺 = to lack (verb) or shortage (noun), specific deficit; 贫乏 = scarce/impoverished (adjective), general insufficiency of resources or content.
造 = make/build/create (general, broad); 制成 = manufacture into a finished product (stresses the end result of production).
遇见 = colloquial chance meeting (person); 遇 = literary/compound base (encounter, also adversity); 逢 = literary every-time-occasion sense; 遭到 = meet with something bad only
接 = go to meet/receive (arranged, pick up); 见 = see/meet (perception-based); 遇见 = chance run-in; 会晤 = formal talks between officials
提 = base verb (carry/mention); 提到 = neutral reference to a topic; 提起 = initiate a topic or stir up (emotionally charged, often formal)
付 = pay/hand over (general); 付款 = make a payment (transaction); 交费 = pay a fee to an institution; 缴纳 = pay obligatory dues/taxes (formal)
配 = actively pair/complement (colors, partners, flavors); 合 = join so parts fit together; 符合 = conform to a standard or requirement
补 = mend by filling/patching (clothes, gaps, deficiencies); 修 = repair colloquially; 修理 = fix a specific broken object; 修复 = restore damaged/historical item
测 = measure/test (general, incl. estimating); 测量 = measure physical dimensions precisely; 调查 = investigate/survey people or facts; 勘探 = prospect/survey terrain for resources
获取 = actively fetch/acquire (information, data, resources — process-focused); 获得 = obtain a result or achievement (outcome-focused, slightly more formal)
预备 = get ready personally (simple preparation, also adj. preparatory); 筹备 = organize and arrange (events, projects — involves coordination)
演 = perform broadly (act,演奏 music, lecture); 表演 = perform for an audience (demonstrate a skill or act); 扮演 = play a specific character/role
促销 = promote sales via discounts/campaigns (commercial); 推广 = popularize/roll out broadly (policy, methods, technology — not necessarily commercial)
练 = practice a skill (general); 练习 = do exercises/drills (educational setting); 操练 = military or physical drill by repetition; 演习 = full-scale military exercise/maneuver
保 = protect/ensure (general base verb); 保护 = safeguard (standard neutral term); 爱护 = care for out of affection (people, things one values); 守护 = stand guard over (vigil, devoted protection)

拔 = pull out forcefully (stuck objects: teeth, nails, weeds); 抽 = extract with sliding motion (cards, draw liquid, smoke); 引 = pull/attract/lead (abstract or directional, guide); 拉 = pull toward oneself (rope, handle, bow strings)
移 = move/shift (general, can be figurative: emigrate, transfer); 挪 = physically nudge/shift a small object to make room (colloquial, concrete)
挑 = pick with a discerning/picky eye (implies fussiness); 拣 = sort and pick out (practical selection); 选拔 = formally select talent or candidates (competitive)
放 = put down/release (general); 放下 = set down and let go (also fig. let go emotionally); 搁 = set aside/shelve (implies leaving it there, often indefinitely)
退出 = exit a place/app/organization; 戒 = quit a habit (smoking, drinking) by self-discipline; 撤退 = military/tactical withdrawal; 退 = back up or return something
认出 = recognize a known person/thing on sight; 认 = recognize or admit (broad base verb); 识别 = identify by category/system; 辨认 = identify by careful scrutiny; 承认 = admit/acknowledge (a fact, guilt)

更换 = swap out (formal, objects); 替 = do something in place of someone; 替代 = substitute (abstract/systemic); 代替 = replace/act for (general); 换 = exchange or switch; 代 = act for + era/generation (multi-sense morpheme)
涨/上涨/涨价 = prices/levels surge (market connotation); 上升/升 = rise physically or in rank; 升高 = rise in measurable degree (temperature, level); 提高 = raise intentionally (quality, standard)
养 = general raise/keep (children or pets); 饲养 = feed and raise animals (husbandry); 抚养 = raise and provide for children (legal/familial); 培养 = nurture/develop people or skills (education context)
返回 = go/travel back (neutral direction); 回来 = come back toward speaker; 归 = return to one's place/origin (literary) or belong to; 归还 = give back a borrowed object
看成/视为 = regard X as Y (equating two things); 视为 is more formal/written; 看待 = treat/view (attitude toward something); 认为 = hold an opinion, think that
剩/剩下 = be left over after use/consumption; 残留 = linger on as a remnant or residue (often negative connotation: pollutant, trace)
记住 = commit to memory (active memorizing); 想起 = recall something previously forgotten; 想到 = think of/realize (may be spontaneous); 记忆 = memory as faculty or stored recollection (noun/verb)
减 = subtract/reduce (math, general morpheme); 减少 = reduce in quantity/amount; 缩小 = shrink in size/scope; 压缩 = compress (file, gas, cut budget tightly); 降低 = bring down a level or standard
接到 = receive a specific communication (call, letter); 接受 = accept/take in (implies willingness); 受到 = be subjected to / undergo (often impact or treatment); 受 = receive or endure (broad base verb)
汇 = remit money (verb morpheme); 汇款 = remittance as noun or verb phrase (more complete collocation used in transactions)
录 = record (audio/video/data); 抄 = hand-copy text (also plagiarize); 复印 = photocopy a document (machine reproduction)
骑车 = ride a bicycle/motorbike (straddle-type); 乘车 = travel by car/vehicle (passenger); 乘/乘坐 = ride/travel by (formal; 乘 also means multiply); 搭 = hitch a ride or take public transport (colloquial)
交换 = swap/exchange objects or roles; 兑换 = exchange currency or redeem vouchers; 交流 = exchange ideas or communicate mutually
欣赏 = appreciate beauty/art/performance (enjoyment); 赏 = admire scenery OR reward/bestow (classical, dual sense); 佩服 = admire someone's skill or character (colloquial respect)
出租 = rent out to someone (landlord/lessor perspective); 租赁 = lease/rental arrangement (neutral legal term, used by either party)
举手 = raise one's hand (specific gesture, e.g. voting or asking); 抬头 = raise one's head (look up); 举 = lift/raise in general or cite an example (broader base verb)
装修 = renovate/fit out a building (construction work); 装饰 = decorate with ornaments (aesthetic embellishment, no structural work)

上升 = general abstract/physical rise; 上涨 = prices/data rising (econ.); 升高 = measurable level rising (temp, water); 涨价 = specifically prices going up (verb-object); 涨 = prices rising (morpheme); 升 = rise/promote (morpheme); 提高 = raise a standard or level (transitive, quality-focused)
加速 = accelerate (physics/formal, rate of speed); 加快 = speed up a pace or progress (broader, less technical)
卖 = everyday sell (colloquial, any context); 出售 = offer for sale (formal/written, e.g. signs); 销售 = sell as a business activity or sales volume (corporate/econ.)
隔/隔开 = separate by physical barrier or interval; 分开 = split apart (general, reversible); 分离 = separate with a sense of parting (often people/components); 分散 = scatter/disperse into multiple parts
搜/搜索 = search by scanning (physical search, internet); 寻找 = look for a specific target; 寻求 = seek/pursue (abstract goals, solutions); 寻觅 = search with longing (literary/emotional)
看见 = catch sight of (emphasises moment of visual perception); 看到 = see/notice (slightly broader, neutral result)
送给 = give as a gift or hand something to someone; 发送 = transmit/send digitally or officially (messages, signals)
签 = sign (a document, general); 签约 = sign a contract (verb-object, commercial/legal); 签证 = visa (noun, admin document bearing official signature/stamp)
唱 = sing (everyday, any context); 演唱 = sing in a formal or stage performance (implies an audience)
吸烟 = smoke (formal/written, e.g. no-smoking signs); 抽烟 = smoke (colloquial everyday speech)
讲 = speak/explain (general, any register); 讲话 = speak or give a speech (semi-formal, implies a speech act); 发言 = make a public statement or address (formal/institutional)
共享 = jointly share/use something (shared resources, infrastructure); 分享 = share with others by passing on or communicating (feelings, content, experiences)
启动 = start/launch (initiate a process: engine, project, system); 帤动 = drive/spur one thing by means of another (cause-effect, pulling effect)
价格 = market price (concrete, monetary); 成本 = production cost/cost price (business/economics); 代价 = price/cost in a figurative sense (sacrifice, consequence)
带动 = to drive/spur (active verb: one thing drives another); 动力 = driving force/impetus (noun: the force or motivation itself)
停 = general stop/park; 停下 = abrupt stop of movement/action (spoken); 停止 = cease (formal, ongoing action); 暂停 = temporary pause (resumes later); 停顿 = brief pause mid-flow (speech, music)
停留 = stop over at a place (movement context); 待 = idle stay, hang around (colloquial); 留 = remain/keep (general, incl. leaving sth behind); 留下 = stay on OR leave sth behind (resultative)
